title: Kasilof River Closed to King Salmon Fishing: What KFF Clients Need to Know
summary: Emergency Order: July 15 – July 31, 2024 (Soldotna) – To protect returning king salmon, the Alaska Department of Fish and Game (ADF&G) has announced the closure of the king salmon fishery on the Kasilof River downstream of the Sterling Highway Bridge. This closure is in effect from 12:01 a.m. Monday...

title: Hutli Pole welcomed at Sandy Beach Park

title: Climate change affecting life in Alaska
summary: Joaqlin Estus ICT In interior Alaska and northwest Canada, temperatures are in the pleasant 60 and 70 degrees Fahrenheit range but climate change is affecting nearly every aspect of life, said Gwich’in Council International board member Evon Taa’ąįį Peter, who is Neetsaii Gwich’in and Koyukon Athabascan. For one thing, he told ICT , more than 60 percent of the forest lands of the Alaskan Gwich’in nation have burned in wildfires in the past 10 years. As of July 10, 582,000 acres had burned this year in 328 wildfires in Alaska....

title: July 2024 Monthly Manager’s Report
summary: July 10, 2024 SUMMARY Greetings! I hope everyone enjoyed a safe and fun-filled holiday celebrating Independence Day with family and friends. This holiday is a wonderful reminder of the freedoms we cherish, and the sacrifices made to secure them. Summer is in full swing, and the Kenai Peninsula is buzzing with activity – it’s the perfect time to make the most of the summer season before it passes. Last month was another busy month, consisting of various business meetings including the Cooperative Finance Corporation (CFC) 2024 Forum in Indianapolis, as well as some personal leave.
